hadiths.ar:
1283  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :   «
إَّلْكُّوق َّه ِ ، وَّ جَّان ِزِ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم . 
ق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب   رجمه إلله: ي عن ى  ن ذ لك  إ ن  ي عز م على مق ام عس زة  إ ي ام ق ى  هذ ة إلموإطن  جي ى ي ي م وي صذي ق  ذ لك  :
hadiths.en:
Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurarah from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir 
{a.s} who said:  
"If a man forgets a prayer or prays it without purification, whether he is at home or 
traveling, and then remembers it, he must make up the prayer that is due, neither 
adding to it nor subtracting from it. If he forgot four rak'ahs (units of prayer), he should 
make up four rak'ahs when he remembers, regardless of whether he is traveling or at 
home. And if  he forgot two rak'ahs, he should perform two rak'ahs when he 
remembers, whether traveling or at home." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 441 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 225 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 1010 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 269 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 118 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1282
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٨٢

hadiths.ar:
1284 - مَّا رَّوَّإةُّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لَّ ن ْن ِ ن َّز ِي غٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ن ِمَّك َّه َّ وَّ 
إَّلْمَّذِي ن َّه ِ ي ُّق َّص َّزُّ إ َّوْ ي ُّي َّم ُّ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"From the reserved rulings is the completion of the prayer in four locations: Mecca, 
Medina, the Mosque of Kufa, and the Shrine (Haa'ir) of Imam Hussain {a.s}."  
[AL SADUQ] 
The author of this book, may Allah {SWT} have mercy upon him, commented: This means 
that one should resolve to stay for ten days in these locations in order to complete (the 
prayer), as confirmed by the following: (continues in the next hadith)  
[REFERENCES] Kamil al-Ziyarat, Part 1, p. 249 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 442 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, 
p. 187 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 531 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 532 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 86, p. 77 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1283
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٨٣

hadiths.ar:
1285  -  وَّ مَّا رَّوَّإةُّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّالِذٍ إَّلْن َّزْق ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ جَّمْز َّة َّ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلْح َّعْق َّزِى ِّ ق َّالَّ: لَّم َّا إ َّن ْ ن َّق زْب ُّ مِن ْ مِن ًى ن َّوَّي ْث ُّ إَّلْمُّق َّامَّ  
ن ِمَّك َّه َّ ق َّا َّي ْمَّمْث ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ ي ُّم َّ ج َّاءَّن ِى  ج َّن َّز  مِن َّ إَّلْمَّن ْز ِلِ ق َّلَّمْ إ َّج ِذْ ن ُّذ إً مِن َّ إَّلْمَّصِي زِ إ ِلَّى إَّلْمَّ ن ْز ِلِ ق َّلَّمْ إ َّذْرِ إ ُّي ِم ُّ إ َّمْ إ ُّق َّصِّزُّ وَّ إ َّن ُّو 
إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّوْمَّن ِذ ٍ ن ِمَّك َّه َّ ق َّا َّي َّي ْي ُّهُّ ق َّق َّصَّصْث ُّ عَّ لَّي ْهِ إَّلْق ِص َّه َّ ق َّق َّالَّ لِى
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi' from Imam al -Ridha {a.s} 
that He said: I asked Imam {a.s} about the prayer in Mecca and Medina, whether it 
should be shortened or completed.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Shorten it unless you intend to stay for ten days”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1284
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٨٤

hadiths.ar:
1286  -  وروى إلق ص ي ل ن ن  ي سار، عن  إ ن ى  عن ذ إلله علي ه إلسلام ق ال: " لي س ق ى  إلسق ز ج معه  ولأ إ ص حى ولأ 
ق طز.
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Khalid al -Barqi from Hamza ibn Abdullah 
al-Ja'fari who said: “When I departed from Mina, I intended to stay in Mecca, so I 
completed the prayer. Then news came from my home, and that I had no choice but 
to return. I was uncertain whether to continue completing my prayers or to shorten 
them. At that time, Abu al-Hasan {a.s} was in Mecca, so I approached him and explained 
the situation.”  
Imam {a.s} said to me: "Return to shortening [your prayers]”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 443 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 221 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 239 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 153 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 509 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1285
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٨٥

hadiths.ar:
1287  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ ِسْمَّاعِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّان ِزٍ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّذْج ُّلُّ عَّلَّى  َّ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلص َّل اَّة ِ وَّ إ َّن َّا ق ِى  إَّلس َّق َّزِ 
ق َّلاَّ إ ُّصَّلِّى  جَّي  َّى إ َّذْج ُّلَّ إ َّهْلِى  ق َّق َّال إَّلص َّلاَّة 
ق َّلاَّ إ ُّصَّلِّى  جَّي  َّى إ َّج ْزُّح َّ ق َّال  عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ .
hadiths.en:
Al-Fudhayl ibn Yasaar reported from Imam Jafar Al -Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"There is no Jumu'ah (Jumu’ah prayer), nor Eid al-Adha, nor Eid al-Fitr for the traveler”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1286
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٨٦

hadiths.ar:
1288  -  وَّ إ َّم َّا ج َّن َّزُّ جَّزِي ز ٍ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ي َّذْج ُّلُّ مِن ْ 
سَّق َّزِةِ وَّ ق َّذْ ذَّج َّلَّ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ وَّ هُّوَّ ق ِى  إَّلط َّزِي ق ِ ق َّال   إ ِلَّى سَّق َّزِةِ وَّ ق َّذْ ذَّج َّلَّ وَّق ْث ُّ 
إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّلْي ُّصَّلِّ إ َّرْن َّعاً .  
ق ا ن ه ي عن ى  ن ه إ ذ إ كان  لأ ي ح اف  ق وإب  ج زوح  إلوق ث  إ ي م وإ ن  ج اف  ج زوح  إلوق ث  ق صز، وي صذي ق  ذ لك  :
hadiths.en:
Ismail ibn Jabir reported: “I said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}: 
"The time for prayer enters while I am traveling, so I do not pray until I return home."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Pray and complete the prayer."  
I said: "The time for prayer enters while I am at home and I intend to travel, so I do not 
pray until I have departed."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Pray and shorten the prayer, for if you do not, you have opposed 
Messenger of Allah {saws}”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 443 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 13 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Part 3, p. 163 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 222 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 240 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 
3, p. 111 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 146 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 512 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 86, p. 46 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1287
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٨٧

hadiths.ar:
1289   -  ق ِى  كِي َّاب ِ إَّلْحَّكَّمِ ن ْن ِ مِسْكِي ن ٍ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّق ْذَّمُّ مِن ْ سَّق َّزِةِ ق ِى  وَّق ْث ِ  
صَّلاَّة ٍ ق َّق َّال .  
وهذ إ موإق ق  لحذي ث  إ سماعي ل ن ن  ج ان ز .
hadiths.en:
Regarding the report of Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Imam Jafar 
ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said:  
"I asked him about a man who returns from his journey while the time for prayer has 
entered, and he is still on the road."  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should pray two rak'ahs (units of prayer). If he sets out on a journey 
and the time for prayer has already entered, then he should pray four rak'ahs."  
[AL SADUQ] 
Indeed, it means that if there is no fear of the time passing, he should complete it. But 
if there is fear of the time passing, he should shorten it. And the proof for this is: (in the 
next hadith) 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 434 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 443 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 13 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 222 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 239 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 145 • Wasail 
Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 513 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 516 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 86, p. 47 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1288
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٨٨

hadiths.ar:
1290 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إ ِسْحَّاق ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ إ َّن َّا إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن َّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم : ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّكُّون ُّ مُّسَّاق ِزإً ي ُّم َّ ي َّق ْذَّمُّ 
ق َّي َّذْج ُّلُّ ن ُّي ُّوب َّ إَّلْكُّوق َّه ِ إ َّ ي ُّي ِم ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ إ َّمْ ي َّكُّون ُّ مُّق َّصِّزإً جَّي  َّى ي َّذْج ُّلَّ إ ِلَّى إ َّهْلِهِ ق َّال ون ُّ مُّق َّصِّزإً جَّي  َّى ي َّذْج ُّلَّ  
إ ِلَّى إ َّهْلِهِ .
hadiths.en:
In the book of Al -Hakam ibn Miskin, it is reported that Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} was asked about a man who arrives from his journey during 
the time for a prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he does not fear that the time for the prayer will expire, he should 
complete it (in full). But if he fears that the time will expire, he should shorten it”.  
[AL SADUQ] 
This is in accordance with the hadith of Ismail ibn Jabir.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 444 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 223 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Part 3, p. 223 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 240 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 241 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 147 • Wasail 
Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 514 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 86, p. 49 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1289
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٨٩

hadiths.ar:
1291  -  وَّ رَّوَّى سَّي ْف   إَّلي  َّم َّارُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: ق َّالَّ لَّهُّ ن َّعْض ُّ إ َّصْحَّان ِن َّا كُّن  َّا ن َّق ْص ِى  صَّلاَّ ة َّ إَّلن  َّهَّارِ إ ِذ َّإ  
ن َّز َّلْن َّا ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ وَّ إَّلْعِس َّاءِ إَّلْأ ج ِزَّة ِ ق َّق َّال ضَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ق َّزَّض َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْمُّسَّاق ِزِ 
رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ لأَّ ق َّن ْلَّهُّمَّا وَّ لأَّ ن َّعْذَّهُّمَّا س َّى ْء  إ ِلأ َّ صَّلاَّة ُّ إَّلل َّي ْلِ عَّلَّى ن َّعِي زِك َّ جَّي ْث ُّ ي َّوَّج  َّهَّ ن ِك َّ .
hadiths.en:
Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} 
about a man who is traveling and then arrives, entering the boundaries of Kufa.  
He asked: "Should he perform the full prayer or continue to shorten it until he enters 
his home?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Rather, he should continue to shorten it until he enters his home”.  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 434 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 444 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 222 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 242 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 142 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 474 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1290
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٠

hadiths.ar:
1292   -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلن  َّاق ِلَّه ِ ن ِالن  َّهَّارِ ق ِى  سَّق َّزٍ ق َّق َّال 
ي َّم َّث ِ إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه ُّ . 
ولأ ن ا س ن ق ص اء صلاة  إللي ل ن الن هار ق ى  إلسق ز .
hadiths.en:
Saif al-Tammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that 
one of our companions said to him:  
"We used to make up the daytime prayers when we would stop between Maghrib 
(evening prayer) and Isha (night prayer)."  
He {a.s} replied: "No, Allah {SWT} knows His servants best when He granted the 
concession. Allah {SWT} the Mighty and Majestic has prescribed for the traveler two 
rak'ahs (units of prayer); there is nothing before or after them except for the night 
prayer while you are on your mount, wherever it may take you”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 445 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 16 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, 
p. 120 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 84 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1291
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩١

hadiths.ar: 1293  - وَّ كَّان َّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ي ُّصَّلِّى  عَّلَّى رَّإجِلَّي ِهِ إَّلْق َّزِي ص َّه َّ ق ِى  ي َّوْمٍ مَّطِي زٍ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about performing the 
supererogatory (nafilah) prayers during the day while traveling.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If it were appropriate to perform the nafilah (supererogatory) prayers 
during travel, then the obligatory (fardh) prayers would have been completed (in full)”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
However, there is no harm in making up the night prayers during the day while 
traveling. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1292
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٢

hadiths.ar:
1294  -  وَّق َّال إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مُّ إَّلْكَّزْج ِى  ُّ :  ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِن ِّى  إ َّق ْذِرُّ إ َّن ْ إ َّي َّوَّج  َّهَّ ن َّحْوَّ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه ِ ق ِى  إَّلْمَّحْمِلِ  
ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} would perform the obligatory prayer on his mount 
on a rainy day. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 445 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 523 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1293
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٣

hadiths.en:
Ibrahim al-Karkhi said:  
"I said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}: "I am able to face the Qiblah 
(direction of prayer) while in the mahmil (a covered mount or carrier)."  
He {a.s} replied: "Is this restriction (truly necessary) ? Do you not have in the Messenger 
of Allah {saws} an example to follow?" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1294
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٤

hadiths.ar:
1296   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ سَّعِي ذُّ ن ْن ي َّسَّارٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّصَّلِّى  صَّلاَّة َّ إَّلل َّي ْلِ وَّ هُّوَّ عَّلَّى ذَّإن  َّي ِهِ إ َّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ 
ي ُّع َّطِّى َّ وَّج ْهَّهُّ وَّ هُّوَّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ق َّال كْس ِق ْهُّ جَّي ْث ُّ مَّا إ َّوْمَّا َّب ْ ن ِهِ 
إَّلذ َّإن  َّه ُّ .
hadiths.en:
Sa'd ibn Sa'd asked Abu al -Hasan al -Ridha {a.s} about a man who is with a 
menstruating woman in a mahmil (covered mount or carrier).  
He asked: "Should he pray while She is with him?"  
He {a.s} replied: "Yes”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1295
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٥

hadiths.ar:
1297  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ِ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّصَّلِّى  إَّلن  َّوَّإق ِلَّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّمْصَّارِ وَّ هُّوَّ 
عَّلَّى ذَّإن  َّي ِهِ جَّي ْي ُّمَّا ي َّوَّج  َّهَّث ْ ن ِهِ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Sa'id ibn Yasarasked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a man 
performing the night prayer while on his mount.  
He asked: "Is it permissible for him to cover his face while praying?"  
He {a.s} replied: "Yes, when he is reciting (the Qur'an). However, when he gestures with 
his face for prostration, he should uncover it wherever the mount takes him”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 446 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 329 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Part 4, p. 422 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1296
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٦

hadiths.ar:
1298 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّق ْطِي ن ٍ ، إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّح ْزُّح ُّ ق ِى  إَّلس َّق َّزِ ي ُّم َّ ي َّن ْذُّو لَّهُّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ ِق َّامَّه ِ وَّ 
هُّوَّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّال ل َّذ ِى  ي َّح ِث ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق ِي هِ إَّلي  َّق ْصِي زُّ 
وَّ إَّلْ أ ِق ْطَّارُّ ق َّال .  
ولأ ن ا س ن الح مغ ن ي ن  إلصلاي ي ن  ق ى  إلسق ز وإلحص ز من  عله  وع ي ز عله . ولأ ن ا س ن ي ا ج ي ز إلمع زب  ق ى  إلسق ز جي ى ي ع ي ث  إلس ق ق .  
ولأ ن ا س ن ي ا ج ي ز إلمع زب  للمساق ز إ ذ إ كان  ق ى  طلث  إلمن ز ل إ لى رن غ إللي ل.
hadiths.en:
Abd al-Rahman ibn al -Hajjaj asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
about a man performing supererogatory (nafilah) prayers in towns and cities while on 
his mount, wherever it faces.  
He {a.s} replied: "There is no harm." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1297
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٧

hadiths.ar:
1299  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال «
ج َّمْسَّه ِ إ َّمْي َّالٍ مِن ْ ن َّعْذِ ع ُّزُّوب ِ إَّلس  َّمْسِ .  
ولأ ن ا س ن ي عح ي ل إلعي مه  ق ى  إلسق ز ق ن ل مع ي ث  إلس ق ق  .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Yaqtin asked Abu al-Hasan {a.s} about a man who sets out on a journey 
and then decides to stay (reside) while he is in the midst of prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should complete (the full prayer) when he decides to stay."  
He also asked about a man who accompanies his brother to the point where 
shortening prayers and breaking the fast becomes obligatory.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in that." 
[AL SADUQ] 
There is also no harm in combining two prayers during travel or when at home, whether 
due to a reason or without a reason. There is no harm in delaying the Maghrib prayer 
during travel until the twilight has disappeared. There is no harm in a traveler dela ying 
Maghrib prayer until one-quarter of the night if he is seeking a place to rest.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1298
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٨

hadiths.ar:
1300  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّم َّار  إَّلس َّان َّاطِى  ُّ ، إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :   عَّن ْ جَّذِّ إَّلطِّي ن ِ إَّل َّذ ِى  لأَّ ي ُّسْح َّذُّ ق ِي هِ مَّا هُّ وَّق َّالَّ  «
ع َّزِق َّث ْ ق ِي هِ إَّلْح َّن ْهَّه ُّ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّي ْن ُّث ْ عَّلَّى إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ .
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Abu Basir, Imam Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "You are in the time of 
Maghrib (evening prayer) while traveling up to a distance of five miles after sunset”.  
[AL SADUQ] 
There is no harm in hastening Isha prayer during travel before the disappearance of 
twilight.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 447 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 234 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 291 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 194 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1299
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٢٩٩

hadiths.en:
Ammar al -Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about the 
extent of wetness or mud that makes it unsuitable for prostration.  
Imam {a.s} said: "When the forehead sinks into it and does not remain firm on the 
ground”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 447 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 312 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Part 2, p. 376 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 143 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 306 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1300
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٣٠٠

hadiths.ar:
1302  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : «
إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ن َّ ط ِلِّ عَّ ي ْزٍ إ ِلَّى ق َّى ْءِ وُّعَّي ْزٍ 
ق َّذ َّرَّعَّي ْهُّ ن َّن ُّو إ ُّمَّي  َّه َّ ي ُّم َّ ج َّز  َّءُّوةُّ عَّلَّى إِي ْن َّى ْ عَّس َّزَّ مِي لاً ق َّكَّان َّ كُّل ُّ مِي لٍ إ َّلْق اً وَّ ج َّمْسَّمِان َّه ِ ذ ِرَّإ عٍ وَّ هُّوَّ إ َّرْن َّعَّه ُّ ق َّزَّإسِح َّ .  
ي عن ى  إ ن ه إ ذ إ كان  إلسق ز إ رن عه  ق زإسح  وإ رإذ إلزج وع من  ي ومه ق الي ق صي ز علي ه وإج ث ، ومي ى لم ي زذ إلزج وع من  ي ومه ق هو 
ن الح ي ار إ ن  س اء إ ي م وإ ن  س اء ق صز وي صذي ق  ما ق سزب  من  ذ لك  :
hadiths.en:
Muawiya ibn Ammar said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}: "The 
people of Mecca complete (perform in full) their prayers at Arafat."  
He {a.s} replied: "Woe to them! (Or, Alas for them!) What journey is more significant than 
this? No, it should not be completed”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 447 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 433 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 487 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 58, H. 1301
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ١٣٠١
